mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.50% and remains there for at least one month, equity risk premiums compress as the cost of capital declines. This monetary accommodation directly increases the present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS=0.12% indicates deep accommodation regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with near-zero rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.50%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) of more than 1% signals deteriorating labor market conditions. This triggers a negative equity risk sentiment shock, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice recession probability.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS=130910k suggests labor market stress if declining
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth above 2% month-over-month indicates robust consumer demand and earnings growth expectations. This sustained demand signal produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions flow through equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS=352985M indicates consumer spending level
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following RSAFS > 2% growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling stagflationary pressure. This joint deterioration in supply capacity and labor utilization produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO=100.3, UNRATE=10.0% suggests potential stagflation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the joint effect of INDPRO and UNRATE deterioration does not explain incremental variance beyond single-series models (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 500k units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2% annualized, indicating demand-side weakness. This dual demand signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 500k units AND GDPC1 growth < 2% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST=574k, GDPC1=12973B suggests moderate demand
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term between HOUST and GDPC1 weakness does not improve predictive power over univariate models (Rank IC improvement < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises more than 0.5% month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, real rates turn deeply negative. This negative real rate regime compresses equity risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors seek real asset protection.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL=217.541, FEDFUNDS=0.12% indicates deeply negative real rates
regime_hint: inflationary environment with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the joint negative real rate signal does not outperform single-series monetary signals (Rank IC difference < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 9.5% and remains elevated for two consecutive months, signaling persistent labor market slack. This sustained high unemployment regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as recession fears intensify and equity risk premiums widen.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the correlation between elevated UNRATE and 5-day negative returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) grows above 1% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 0.3% month-over-month, indicating supply-side expansion without demand-pull inflation. This benign supply expansion produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as profit margins expand and earnings growth accelerates.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO grows > 1% month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL rises < 0.3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO=100.3, CPIAUCSL=217.541 suggests moderate supply and price conditions
regime_hint: goldilocks supply expansion with contained inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the joint supply-inflation signal does not improve predictive power over INDPRO alone (Rank IC improvement < 0.025).
